content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN. For a permutation PPP of the integers 111 to NNN, let f(P,A)f(P,A)f(P,A) denote the sum of the minimum values of AiA_iAi​ within each cycle of the set of permutation cycles of PPP.

A permutation cycle is a cycle in the permutation graph, where the nodes represent the indices 111 to NNN, and the edges connect node iii to node PiP_iPi​. If PPP is a permutation, each connected component is a cycle.

For example, for the permutation P=[2,4,5,1,3]P = [2, 4, 5, 1, 3]P=[2,4,5,1,3] and the array A=[3,1,5,2,10]A = [3,1,5,2,10]A=[3,1,5,2,10], the cycles are (1,2,4)(1, 2, 4)(1,2,4) and (3,5)(3,5)(3,5).

To calculate f(P,A)f(P,A)f(P,A), we find the minimum value of AiA_iAi​ within each cycle of the permutation PPP, and then sum these minimum values. For the given example:


For the cycle (1,2,4)(1, 2, 4)(1,2,4), the minimum value of A1A_1A1​, A2A_2A2​, and A4A_4A4​ is 111.
For the cycle (3,5)(3,5)(3,5), the minimum value of A3A_3A3​ and A5A_5A5​ is 555.
So, f(P,A)=min⁡(A1,A2,A4)+min⁡(A3,A5)=1+5=6f(P,A) = \min(A_1,A_2,A_4) + \min(A_3,A_5) = 1 + 5 = 6f(P,A)=min(A1​,A2​,A4​)+min(A3​,A5​)=1+5=6.

Define g(A)g(A)g(A) to be the sum of f(P,A)f(P,A)f(P,A) over all N!N!N! permutations PPP of the integers 111 to NNN.

Answer QQQ queries of the following form:


Given iii and xxx, find g(A)g(A)g(A) if we set Ai:=xA_i := xAi​:=x.
Note that updates are not persistent across queries — that is, for each query, start with the original array AAA, update the value of a single index, and compute the answer.

The answer can be large, so compute it modulo MMM.

Note that MMM is **not necessarily prime.**


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, QQQ and MMM — the length of the array, number of queries and the modulo, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of array AAA.
The next QQQ lines describe the queries. Each of them contains contains two space-separated integers iii and xxx.




Output Format
For each test case, output QQQ lines. The iii-th of them should be the answer to the iii-th query, modulo MMM.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
